question: What support do you provide? How do we escalate critical issues?
title: Support Model, Escalation Paths, and Operational Responsiveness
bullets: Define support tiers, channels (email, portal, phone, Slack), and coverage (business hours vs 24/7)
Specify response and resolution SLAs by severity (P0–P3) with clear definitions
Describe escalation ladder (on-call, management, executive) and contact mechanisms
Provide evidence of support quality (MTTR, CSAT/NPS, real-time communication practices)
suggested_inputs: Support policy document with severity definitions and SLAs
Escalation matrix and on-call schedule/contact methods
Support performance metrics (MTTR by severity, CSAT/NPS)
Examples of incident communications and post-incident reports

good.text: Support tiers: Standard (business hours via email/portal), Premium (24/7 coverage via phone/Slack, dedicated CSM). P0 incidents receive immediate response with on-call engineers engaged. Channels and coverage are clearly defined.

Severity levels (P0–P3) are explicitly defined with response and resolution SLAs (e.g., P0 ≤1 hour response, continuous work until resolution). Ownership and decision criteria for severity are documented.

Escalation path includes on-call engineer → incident commander → senior engineering leadership → executive escalation (e.g., VP/CTO) for P0. Direct contact methods are provided for off-hours critical incidents.

We track MTTR by severity, CSAT/NPS, and publish incident updates via status page and dedicated channels (e.g., Slack bridge). Post-incident RCAs and regular reviews (QBRs) ensure continuous improvement.

partial.text: Support is available via email/portal during business hours, with limited after-hours coverage for critical issues.
We prioritize issues by severity, but definitions and SLAs are not consistently documented or communicated.
Escalation is possible internally, but customers are not provided with a clear escalation ladder or direct contacts.
We track support performance internally, but do not share MTTR or customer satisfaction metrics with customers.

bad.text: Support is primarily provided via a community forum without dedicated response commitments.
Support is limited to email during business hours, even for critical issues.
An AI chatbot handles most support requests without escalation to human engineers.
There is no clearly defined escalation process for critical incidents.

follow_ups: Provide the escalation contact list: who do we call at 2am on Sunday for a P0 incident?
Define P0/P1/P2 severities and who determines classification during incidents.
What is your mean time to resolve (MTTR) by severity over the last 12 months?
Can we test responsiveness by contacting support now?
Provide customer references who can speak to support quality and incident handling.
